title: What is Java?
text: Based on the training document, what is Java?
correctAnswerText: Java is a high-level, class-based, object-oriented programming language that is designed to have as few implementation dependencies as possible. It is a general-purpose programming language intended to let programmers write once, run anywhere, meaning that compiled Java code can run on all platforms that support Java without the need to recompile.
hint: Think about its implementation dependencies and write-once-run-anywhere philosophy.

title: JDK, JRE, JVM Differences
text: Explain the difference between JDK, JRE, and JVM according to the provided material.
correctAnswerText: JDK (Java Development Kit) is a software development environment that includes JRE and development tools like a compiler and debugger. JRE (Java Runtime Environment) provides a runtime environment for running software. JVM (Java Virtual Machine) executes the compiled Java bytecode.
hint: What does each acronym stand for and what is their primary use?

title: Primary use of JDK
text: What does the JDK include that the JRE does not?
options: A. Only the Java Virtual Machine
B. Development tools like a compiler and debugger
D. The Java heap memory manager
correctAnswerText: JDK includes development tools like a compiler (javac), debugger, and documentation generator, whereas JRE only provides the runtime environment.

title: Document Base Code
text: Write a simple Java application consisting of a single main class that prints 'Hello World'. This reflects the basic setup taught in the document.
hint: Standard public static void main declaration.
